rationale: All four events (A0, EV-001, EV-002, EV-003) are Windows Security event 5145 (network share object access) against SYSVOL, which is routine domain-joined behavior: EV-001 and EV-002 show host-01$ (the machine account) reading well-known default GPO gpt.ini files ({31B2F340} and {6AC1786C} are the Default Domain Policy and Default Domain Controllers Policy GUIDs) via a link-local IPv6 address, consistent with normal Group Policy refresh; A0 and EV-003 show user-02$ (another machine account) enumerating corp.local\Policies and its root over a private IPv4 address (10.0.1.4), also consistent with GP processing. Access masks 0x120089 (read/synchronize) and 0x100081 (read attributes/synchronize) are read-only and non-destructive. No write, modify, or suspicious enumeration pattern is evidenced, no lateral movement artifacts, no anomalous tooling, and the subjects are machine accounts performing expected SYSVOL reads.
